title: Battle of Rendili
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Battle |prev=[[Duel on the Titavian IV|Duel on the ''Titavian IV'']][[Republic 70|''Republic'' 70]] |conc= |next=*[[Duel in the Coruscant Underlevels]][[Republic 71|''Republic'' 71]] *[[Battle of Boz Pity (Clone Wars)|Battle of Boz Pity]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *Eventually the [[Malevolence Campaign/Legends|''Malevolence'' Campaign]] |image=[[File:Triumph_and_Doneeta.jpg]] |name=Battle of Rendili |conflict=[[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] |date=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]{{FactFile2014|23|PLO 1-6|Plo Koon}} puts the Battle of Rendili prior to "[[Shadow of Malevolence]]", as part of the compression and rearranging of the pre-''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' timeline of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. As "Shadow of Malevolence" is dated to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] by {{FactFile2014|24|22 BBY 87-88|The ''Malevolence'' Campaign}}, the Battle of Rendili must also occur in that year. |casual2=*One ''Lucrehulk''-class Core Ship *Vulture [[Droid starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|You really have done superb work, Anakin. Saving the Rendili Fleet! Far better than I could have hoped.|Supreme Chancellor Palpatine|}} The '''Battle of Rendili''' was a battle of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] that occurred in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. ==Prelude== When word came that [[Rendili/Legends|Rendili]], the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] home to the [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]] corporation and producer of the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]], was possibly going to join the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] ordered a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-led task force to Rendili. Their mission was either to convince the [[Rendili Home Defense Fleet]] to remain with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] or, if that failed, to destroy them lest they fell into the hands of [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. As the commander of the Rendili Fleet was [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jace Dallin]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] and Captain [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] were sent as part of the fleet to negotiate with him, the three having fought together during the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]]. Arriving at Rendili, the Republic force discovered that an [[Independent Provisional Government of Rendili|Independent Provisional Government]] had already assumed power on Rendili, and joined with the Separatists. Koon and Dodonna headed over to the Rendili [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], ''[[Mersel Kebir]]'', to speak with Dallin anyways. Despite some initial reluctance, Dallin agreed to order the fleet to defect to the Republic. At that time, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Mellor Yago]] with the help of other junior officers such as [[Gallan]] arrested Koon, Dodonna, Dallin, and the other senior officers of the ''Mersel Kebir'' for [[Treason/Legends|treason]] against Rendili. Yago used them as hostages preventing an immediate attack, hoping that [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]], commanding the Republic fleet, would hold off firing for at least twenty hours—the time at which [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] had promised a [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate Navy]] support group would arrive at Rendili. ===Interlude on the ''Titavian''=== Just before the negotiating team crossed to the ''Mersel Kebir'' [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], one of the Jedi assigned to the Republic fleet, was asked by the [[Exploration Corps/Legends|Exploration Corps]] to investigate a missing ship for the [[Mycroft]] [[Intergalactic Zoological Society]]. Going to the coordinates given, he found the crippled ship, ''[[Titavian IV]]'', with an old Jedi signal coming from it. Expecting perhaps to find [[Zao]] or [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] onboard, Kenobi instead found [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], a renegade Jedi who had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and defected to Dooku. Vos was being hunted by seeker droids released by two of Dooku's minions, [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] and [[Tol Skorr]], who were charged with punishing Vos for his failure following the [[Battle of Honoghr/Legends|Battle of Honoghr]], where he had realized that he had indeed followed the wrong path. Stranded on the ''Titavian IV'' when the two [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] had placed his ship, the ''[[Skorp-Ion]]'', out of reach, Vos had nearly been overwhelmed before Kenobi's arrival. Retaking Vos' ship, evading the Dark Jedi's droids and a [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] released by them, and destroying their own ship, the two Jedi reversed the situation, escaping while leaving Ventress and Skorr stranded onboard the ''[[Titavian IV]]''. Kenobi piloted their ship back to Rendili, despite the fact he anticipated trouble from Tiin over Vos. ==The battle== {{Quote|The idiots are going to force us to kill them.|Saesee Tiin|}} [[File:GreenTwo-SWR70.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Republic starfighters engage the droid fighters of the Confederacy]] Meanwhile, Yago—expecting the arrival of the Confederate support group at any minute—had given Tiin an ultimatum: in one hour he would execute his hostages, starting with Koon. Onboard the Republic flagship, ''[[Sundiver]]'', Tiin and Skywalker debated what to do. Deciding that there was no way to save the hostages, Tiin wished to destroy the Rendili Fleet ships, but Skywalker was convinced that in his [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], the ''[[Azure Angel II]]'', he could set charges on the rear of the cruisers, leaving them stranded in space via a flaw in their original [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] design. Tiin refused, as did Kenobi when he arrived shortly later with Vos. Tiin ordered Vos confined in a prison cell as [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Adar Tallon/Legends|Adar Tallon]], the Naval commander, reported that Confederate [[Core ship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Core Ships]] had emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Ordering the ''Sundiver'' to begin firing on the ''Mersel Kebir'' and the [[Triumph (Acclamator-class)|''Triumph'']] and ''[[Doneeta]]'' to fire on the Confederate ships, Tiin and the other Jedi headed to their starfighters to enter the battle. Without permission, Anakin had the charges loaded onto his fighter and prepared to put his plan in motion. When the ''Sundiver'' was hit and suffered a power loss, Vos was able to leave his cell and get onboard a starfighter, entering the battle. Together with Kenobi, they infiltrated the ''Mersel Kebir'' as a means of destroying its Vulture droids from within, giving the distraction Skywalker needed to pull his plan through. Yago, in preparation for a hyperspace jump, had all the Rendili ships slave themselves to the ''Mersel Kebir''; when sensors revealed this, Tiin ordered Tallon to ram the ''Sundiver'' into the ''Mersel Kebir'', but this was made unnecessary when Skywalker succeeded in disabling the astrogation of the ''Mersel Kebir'', leaving it and the other Rendili Fleet ships slaved to it stranded. On board the Rendili flagship, Vos and Kenobi had managed to release the hostages. Dallin personally shot Yago, and retook command of the Rendili Fleet. Ordering that all other mutineers be summarily executed, he and Tiin ordered their combined fleets to concentrate fire on the Confederate ships, destroying one of them and he other retreating, ending the battle. ==Aftermath==
[[File:Rendili_Coruscant.jpg|thumb|300px|The Rendili Fleet is delivered to Coruscant.]]
After the surrender of the fleet, the Independent Provisional Government was presumably quickly subdued as Rendili returned to the Republic by the end of the war. Following the battle, the Rendili Fleet was taken to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to be refitted and used in the war effort. Due to their strong design, Supreme Chancellor Palpatine planned to turn them into prison ships for use in transporting captured Dark Jedi loyal to Dooku. Presumably they became some of the Imperial [[Prison ship/Legends|dungeon ships]] used during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], alongside the [[Lictor-class dungeon ship|''Lictor''-class]]. At Palpatine's urging, the Senate passed a resolution that mandated all defense fleets to be assimilated into the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]], to prevent a similar situation from arising in the future. This influx of power into the central military while weakening local systems would form the basis of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] ability to "[[Tarkin Doctrine/Legends|rule through threat of force rather than force itself]]." Ventress and Skorr were rescued from the ''Titavian IV'' by General Grievous who arrived from the battle aboard the surviving Core Ship. Skorr was taken by Grievous to make an accounting of the fiasco to Dooku, while Ventress made her way to Coruscant to deal with Vos. When she saw Skywalker, she changed her tactics and followed him. She witnessed one of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala's]] [[Handmaiden/Legends|handmaidens]] bring him a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of his wife, and showed herself suddenly, threatening to kill the "Jedi toy" before finishing him. In a fit of anger, [[Duel in the Coruscant Underlevels|Skywalker fought Ventress]], sustaining a permanent scar to his face before wrapping her in electrical cables and throwing her off a tower into the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]]. She was presumed dead, but would later be [[Battle of Boz Pity (Clone Wars)|found]] to be alive. At a meeting before the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], Vos was cleared of all suspicion and welcomed back into the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi Order]]. They failed to realize that his true allegiance still remained with Dooku, who had engineered the entire situation as a means to get a spy into the Jedi ranks. ==Behind the scenes== The novel ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' includes a scene where [[Whie Malreaux/Legends|Whie Malreaux]] sees a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] representation of the "Rendili Fleet Crisis" in the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. However, ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the battle of Rendili ''after'' the [[Mission to Vjun (Clone Wars)|Mission to Vjun]] due to the fact that in the novel, Asajj Ventress is known to be alive and well (she was seen by Whie and Scout which would have been revealed in their mission report). In ''Obsession'', however, Anakin is convinced that Ventress is dead (he believes he killed her at the end of the comics miniseries [[Star Wars: Republic: Dreadnaughts of Rendili|''The Dreadnaughts of Rendili'']]). Thus, it has to be assumed that the "Rendili crisis" mentioned in the novel (which is referred to only as a 'crisis' and not a 'battle') must be a reference to negotiations that preceded the later armed conflict seen in the comic. In the [[Republic 69|first issue]] of the ''The Dreadnaughts of Rendili'' story arc, Anakin is shown arriving in the midst of these negotiations. Due to the continuity issues introduced by the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' series, much of the pre-[[2009]] timeline of the Clone Wars was compressed into the first few weeks of the conflict, and [[The Official Star Wars Fact File Part 23|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' Part 23]] later definitively placed the Battle of Rendili prior to the events of the series, thus moving it to 22 BBY. ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'', meanwhile, was moved to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] by ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''. ==Appearances== *[[Republic 70|''Republic'' 70]] {{1st}} *[[Republic 71|''Republic'' 71]] *''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Republic 78|''Republic'' 78]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SideshowCite|set=Militaries of Star Wars|packtype=Sixth Scale Figure|pack=Commander Cody (212th Attack Battalion)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/SSC-Cody.asp}} *{{DB|character|commandercody|Cody, Clone Commander}} *{{DB|character|saeseetiin|Tiin, Saesee}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *{{FactFile2014|23|PLO 1-6|Plo Koon}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Rendili |es=Batalla de Rendili |pl=Bitwa o Rendili |plFA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Rendili, Battle of}} [[Category:Battles in the Core Worlds]] [[Category:Battles of the Clone Wars]]
